title: Richly Spiced Chinese Pork Noodles
description: A floral and indulgent Chinese dish featuring garam masala and pickle, made with whole30-friendly ingredients, this recipe combines the richness of pork and noodles with a cheese-pull texture.
instructions: Heat the sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the pork shoulder and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the garam masala and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped pickle and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the coconut aminos and stir to combine.
Add the zucchini noodles and cook until they start to soften, about 3 minutes.
Transfer the noodles to a serving dish and top with green onions.
Garnish with additional green onions and pickle if desired.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
